docs.title: DonaDataset
docs.text:
DonaDataset is the annotated camera-trap image dataset used to train DonaNet, a YOLO-based neural network for detecting and classifying the mammals that inhabit Doñana National Park (Spain).

Images and labels are hosted on HuggingFace Hub and also published in the Arias Montano institutional repository of the University of Huelva, on Zenodo, and on Dataverse. This repository contains the metadata, class definitions, and utility scripts.

docs.title: Documentation Map
docs.text:
• Dataset Description
  • Classes
  • Collection Protocol
  • About
  • Publishing Guide ← for maintainers

docs.title: Quick download
docs.text:
python scripts/download.py

Or with the HuggingFace CLI:

huggingface-cli download wildintelproject/donadataset \
  --repo-type dataset --local-dir ./data

docs.title: DonaDataset & WildINTEL
docs.text:
DonaDataset is the training dataset behind DonaNet, a YOLO-based neural network developed as part of the WildINTEL project, an initiative dedicated to the automated monitoring of wildlife through artificial intelligence and camera-trap imagery.

The dataset focuses on the mammal species that inhabit Doñana National Park (Spain), one of the most important wetland ecosystems in Europe.

docs.title: Funding
docs.text:
This work is part of the WildINTEL project, funded by the Biodiversa+ Joint Research Call 2022–2023 "Improved transnational monitoring of biodiversity and ecosystem change for science and society (BiodivMon)".

Biodiversa+ is the European co-funded biodiversity partnership supporting excellent research on biodiversity with an impact for policy and society. Biodiversa+ is part of the European Biodiversity Strategy for 2030 that aims to put Europe's biodiversity on a path to recovery by 2030 and is co-funded by the European Commission.

docs.title: Classes
docs.text:
The dataset covers the following mammal species present in Doñana National Park. Class ids match the order in metadata/classes.yaml and metadata/dataset.yaml.

ID Common name Scientific name 0 Red deer Cervus elaphus 1 Fallow deer Dama dama 2 Wild boar Sus scrofa 3 Iberian lynx Lynx pardinus 4 Red fox Vulpes vulpes 5 Egyptian mongoose Herpestes ichneumon 6 European rabbit Oryctolagus cuniculus 7 Badger Meles meles

Adding new classes

To extend the dataset with new species, add entries to metadata/classes.yaml keeping ids contiguous, update metadata/dataset.yaml accordingly, and open a pull request. The CI workflow will verify consistency automatically.

docs.title: Camera traps
docs.text: Images were collected using camera traps deployed across Doñana National Park. Cameras were positioned at known wildlife corridors, water points and feeding areas to maximise species coverage.

docs.title: Image processing
docs.text: Raw images are exported in JPEG format. No colour correction or resizing is applied before annotation — the model receives images at their original resolution.

docs.title: Annotation workflow
docs.text:
Annotations were produced using the YOLO bounding-box format. Each detected animal is labelled with:

  • Class id — species identifier (see Classes)
  • Bounding box — normalised centre coordinates and dimensions

Ambiguous detections (partial occlusion, low confidence) are excluded from the dataset.

docs.title: Dataset splits
docs.text:
Images are split into train / val / test partitions using the DonaNet prepare-dataset command with the following default ratios:

Split Ratio train 70 % val 20 % test 10 %

Stratified sampling is applied to ensure each species is represented in all splits.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: DonaDataset is a collection of camera-trap images annotated in YOLO format for object detection. It covers the mammal species present in Doñana National Park (Huelva, Spain), one of the most important wetland ecosystems in Europe.

docs.title: Storage
docs.text: Images and labels are hosted on HuggingFace Hub at wildintelproject/donadataset, published in the Arias Montano institutional repository of the University of Huelva, on Zenodo, and on Dataverse.

docs.title: Dataset splits
docs.text: Split Purpose train Model training val Hyperparameter tuning / monitoring test Final evaluation (held-out)

docs.title: Directory layout (after download)
docs.text:
data/
├── train/
│   ├── images/   ← camera-trap images (.jpg)
│   └── labels/   ← YOLO annotations (.txt)
├── val/
│   ├── images/
│   └── labels/
└── test/
    ├── images/
    └── labels/

docs.title: Annotation format
docs.text:
Each label file contains one row per detected animal:

<class_id> <x_center> <y_center> <width> <height>

All coordinates are normalised to [0, 1] relative to the image dimensions.

docs.title: Metadata files
docs.text: File Description metadata/classes.yaml Maps class ids to common and scientific species names metadata/dataset.yaml Ultralytics YOLO config — use directly with donanet train

docs.title: Publishing Guide
docs.text: This document explains how to publish and keep DonaDataset synchronized across all external repositories. It is aimed at the dataset maintainer.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Repository Type DOI Audience HuggingFace Hub Specialised (ML) No AI / ML community Zenodo Open science archive Yes Scientific community Dataverse Research data repository Yes Scientific community Arias Montano (UHU) Institutional repository Yes University of Huelva

docs.title: What is stored where
docs.text: What Where Images + labels (the actual data) HuggingFace Hub Code + metadata + documentation This GitHub repository → archived in Zenodo (with DOI) Academic / citable record Arias Montano (UHU), Dataverse

docs.title: 1. HuggingFace Hub
docs.text: URL: https://huggingface.co/datasets/wildintelproject/donadataset

docs.title: First-time setup
docs.text:
1. Create an account at huggingface.co and join the wildintelproject organisation.
  2. Install the HuggingFace CLI: 
    pip install huggingface-hub
huggingface-cli login   # paste your access token
  3. Create the dataset repository on the web: New → Dataset → wildintelproject/donadataset (set to Public, CC BY 4.0).

docs.title: Uploading images and labels
docs.text:
# Upload a specific split (e.g. train)
huggingface-cli upload wildintelproject/donadataset ./data/train train \
  --repo-type dataset

# Upload all splits at once
for split in train val test; do
  huggingface-cli upload wildintelproject/donadataset ./data/$split $split \
    --repo-type dataset
done

docs.title: Updating the dataset card
docs.text: The dataset card is the README.md inside the HuggingFace repository (not this GitHub repo). Edit it directly on the HuggingFace web UI or push a README.md via the CLI.

docs.title: On every new version
docs.text:
1. Upload the new/updated images and labels as above.
  2. Update the version tag in the dataset card.
  3. Update metadata/dataset.yaml in this GitHub repo if splits or class IDs changed.

docs.title: 2. Zenodo
docs.text:
URL: https://zenodo.org

⚠️ What Zenodo hosts: Zenodo archives the contents of this GitHub repository (metadata, scripts, documentation). The images and labels are NOT stored in Zenodo — they live on HuggingFace Hub. The Zenodo deposit exists to provide a citable DOI for the dataset and to ensure long-term preservation of the code and metadata.

docs.title: First-time setup — GitHub integration (recommended)
docs.text:
1. Log in at zenodo.org with your GitHub account.
  2. Go to Account → GitHub.
  3. Find wildintelproject/donadataset and toggle it ON.
  4. Zenodo will now watch this repository for new releases.

docs.title: Publishing a new version
docs.text:
1. In this GitHub repository, create a new release: Releases → Draft a new release → Create tag (e.g. v1.1.0) → Publish release.
  2. Zenodo automatically creates a new deposit and mints a DOI within a few minutes.
  3. Go to the Zenodo deposit, review the metadata (title, authors, licence, description), and click Publish if it is not published automatically.
  4. Copy the DOI badge URL and update it in README.md: 
    [![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX)

Note: Zenodo archives the GitHub repository contents (code + metadata), not the images themselves (those live on HuggingFace). Include a clear note in the Zenodo description pointing to HuggingFace for the actual data files.

docs.title: 3. Dataverse
docs.text: URL: https://dataverse.harvard.edu (or another Dataverse instance if preferred)

docs.title: First-time setup
docs.text:
1. Create an account at dataverse.harvard.edu.
  2. Request access to or create a Dataverse collection for WildINTEL / University of Huelva.
  3. Click Add Data → New Dataset.

docs.title: Filling in the metadata
docs.text: Field Value Title DonaDataset: Camera-trap mammal dataset from Doñana National Park Author WildINTEL project team Contact (project contact email) Description (copy from docs/dataset-description.md) Subject Earth and Environmental Sciences Licence CC BY 4.0 Related publication (add DOI of associated paper when available)

docs.title: Uploading files
docs.text:
• Upload the image archives and label files directly through the web UI, or use the Dataverse API: 
    curl -H "X-Dataverse-key: $DATAVERSE_API_TOKEN" \
     -X POST \
     -F "file=@data/train.zip" \
     "https://dataverse.harvard.edu/api/datasets/:persistentId/add?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/XXXXXX"
  • Add metadata/classes.yaml and metadata/dataset.yaml as supplementary files.

docs.title: On every new version
docs.text:
1. Open the existing dataset on Dataverse.
  2. Click Edit Dataset → Add New Files and upload the updated data.
  3. Increment the version number and click Publish.

docs.title: 4. Arias Montano (University of Huelva)
docs.text:
URL: https://rabida.uhu.es

Arias Montano is the institutional open-access repository of the University of Huelva, managed by the university library service. Deposits are made by request.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Contact the Biblioteca de la Universidad de Huelva to open a deposit:
  2. Web: https://www.uhu.es/biblioteca/
  3. Email: biblioteca@uhu.es
  4. Provide the following information:
  5. Title, authors, abstract (in Spanish and English).
  6. Licence: CC BY 4.0.
  7. Type of resource: Dataset.
  8. Links to HuggingFace Hub and Zenodo (DOI).
  9. Associated publication or project (WildINTEL / Biodiversa+).
  10. The library will assign a permanent handle/URI and confirm the deposit.
  11. Update README.md replacing the generic Arias Montano link with the specific record URL.

docs.title: On every new version
docs.text: Contact the library again to add a new version record linked to the existing deposit.

docs.title: Checklist for a new dataset release
docs.text:
• [ ] Upload new images and labels to HuggingFace Hub.
  • [ ] Update metadata/classes.yaml and metadata/dataset.yaml if needed.
  • [ ] Create a GitHub release (triggers Zenodo automatically).
  • [ ] Review and publish the Zenodo deposit; update the DOI badge in README.md.
  • [ ] Upload new files to Dataverse and publish the new version.
  • [ ] Notify Arias Montano library if the update is significant.
  • [ ] Update the version number in README.md and docs/dataset-description.md.
